response.justification: Bogdan Szabo brings over 17 years of broad engineering experience and demonstrable depth in high-throughput backend systems, but the resume shows no evidence of the core domain this role requires: experimentation platforms, A/B testing infrastructure, feature flagging, telemetry pipelines, or statistical analysis for experiment quality. The Principal/IC6 level demands proven ownership of foundational cross-team systems in the growth and experimentation space, which is entirely absent from his history. His strongest signal—low-latency micro-services at Dunnhumby handling millions of requests per second—is directionally relevant but insufficient to bridge the domain gap.
strengths: 17+ years of engineering across embedded, mobile, backend, and distributed systems with genuine polyglot breadth (C/C++, Java, TypeScript, D)
Hands-on experience with high-throughput, low-latency infrastructure at Dunnhumby processing millions of HTTP requests per second
Staff-level and founding roles showing ability to operate with autonomy and mentor others across multiple organizations
concerns: Zero mention of experimentation platforms, A/B testing, feature flagging, or rollout-risk tooling—the core deliverables of this role
No telemetry, metrics instrumentation, or data/statistical analysis experience visible anywhere in the resume
No demonstrated Principal/IC6-scope ownership: cross-team platform initiatives, unified measurement systems, or multi-workstream technical leadership at scale

key_factors.factor: No telemetry or metrics infrastructure background
